identifier: IBM Systems Journal, vol. 9, no. 2, pp. 78-117, doi:10.1147/sj.92.0078
summary: Introduces stack processing, a one-pass technique for evaluating demand-paged storage hierarchies, and formalizes the class of stack replacement algorithms with the inclusion property. Least-recently-used (LRU) replacement is the canonical stack algorithm analyzed: pages are ordered by recency of reference and the least recently used page is evicted when a fixed-capacity level is full. This paper is the standard scholarly reference for LRU eviction semantics and its capacity-inclusion behavior.
title: Evaluation Techniques for Storage Hierarchies
